info.name: BioStar X API
info.description: ## Overview Suprema BioStar X API is a JSON-based API. All requests are made to endpoints beginning with [https://server_ip|domain_name[:port]/api](https://server_ip|domain_name:port/api) The API can be used to perform almost any operation. Following are a few examples of what can be done with Suprema BioStar X API: - Manage User data - Manage Access Group data - Manage Doors data - Assign an Access Group to one or multiple User - Assign a User with one or multiple Access Group - Manage Devices - And many more... All requests must be secure (https). ## General Information To call the API, BioStar X is mainly using HTTP Methods `GET`, `POST`, `PUT`, and `DELETE`. - `GET` is used to retrieve data from the server at the specified resource. For example, if you want to retrieve the User data with /users endpoint, then by making a `GET` request to the endpoint should result with the list of all available users. - `POST` request is used to send data to the server to create or update a resource. The data sent to the server is stored on the body of the HTTP request. - `PUT` is similar to `POST` in that it creates or updates a resource. The difference between them is that when calling the same `PUT` requests multiple times it will have the same result. While calling a `POST` request repeatedly will create the same resource multiple times. - `DELETE` is used to delete resources at the specified endpoint. There are four parameter location that’s being used on Suprema BioStar X API: - PATH parameter is located on the URL, usually on the end of an endpoint. For example in /items/{ItemID}, the PATH parameter is ItemID. - BODY. An array of string values will be located on the BODY of the HTTP request. The request body needs to be in JSON format. - QUERY. Similar to the PATH parameter, QUERY parameter is also located on the URL, the difference is QUERY parameter will be located after a question mark. Example: /items?id=###, the QUERY parameter is id. - HEADER. Custom headers that are expected as part of the request. Path templating refers to the usage of template expressions, delimited by curly braces ({}), to mark a section of a URL path as replaceable using path parameters. Example: [https://127.0.0.1:4433/api/some_api/{id](https://127.0.0.1:4433/api/some_api/%7Bid)} Above example shows that the required value will be placed on {id} For `AUTHORIZATION` details please check `POST Login` documentation. ## Import the Postman collection To import the Postman collection online, follow these steps: 1. Download the Postman collection file(bsxapi-postman-collection.json). 2. Go to Postman and sign in. 3. Select your workspace from the workspace selector in the top left. Or create a workspace if you don't have one. 4. Click **Import** in the sidebar. 5. Select downloaded file, or drag the file into the import window. 6. Click **Import** to complete the process.

item.name: Access Group

request.description:
This request is used to make a new Access Group (AG)

#### BODY Parameters:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| name | String | Y | The name of the AG. Must be unique |
| description | String | N | AG's description |
| users | Number | N | List of user(s) id of the AG |
| user_group | Number | N | List of user group(s) id of the AG |
| access_levels | Number | N | List of access level(s) id of the AG |
| floor_levels | Number | N | List of floor level(s) id of the AG |

If BioStar X does not have license for Elevator module, skip the **floor_levels** parameter or put it like this: **"floor_levels": ""**

For numeric value (such as id's), the value can be put on a quote or not.

#### Response Properties:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| AccessGroup | Object | AccessGroup object |
| AccessGroup.id | Number | ID of the Access Group which was created |
| AccessGroup.name | String | Name of the Access Group which was created |
| DeviceResponse | Object | DeviceResponse object |
| DeviceResponse.rows | Array | List of device responses |
| DeviceResponse.id | Number | ID of the device which updated it's AG. |
| DeviceResponse.code | Number | Response code from the device. 0=Success |
| DeviceResponse.result | Boolean | Summary of device response. True when all devices successfully updated (code=0), False when even one is failed (code != 0). |

#### Known errors:

- Placing "/" at the end of the endpoint
    
- `name` is a required and unique parameter

To add or delete User Groups, put new ones in this field or remove what you want to delete from the field. | | access_levels | Array | N | List of access level(s) id of the AG | | floor_levels | Array | N | List of floor level(s) id of the AG | | new_users | Array | N | List of user(s) id which will be added to the AG | | delete_users | Array | N | List of user(s) id which will be deleted from the AG | #### Notes: - To add/delete multiple, repeat the braces (see users example) - When you add/update data, it will remove any previous data and add the one that you input. Ex: If parameter description have value, and on update this parameter value is left empty, then the previous value will be removed. - To skip a record from updating, either skip the parameter or fill the current data on the update field. - When updating an AG, parameter `user_group` need to be filled if existing User Group want to be retained. If this parameter is left empty, the existing User Group(s) in the AG that should be still there will not be there. #### Known Errors: - Repeating the same value on parameter with multiple options

request.description: This API is used to view BioStar X Backup configuration.

This API is used to check if there's any running backup process.

Message response will be True if there's any, and False if there's no running backup process.

This API is used to modify BioStar X backup configuration.

#### Parameters:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| use | String | Y | Toggle true to use Shceduled Backup |
| cycle | String | Y | Number of backup files to keep (1 to 100) |
| path | String | Y | Folder path, make sure that the folder exist |
| minute | String | Y | Minute value for Scheduled Backup (0 to 59) |
| hour | String | Y | Hour value for Scheduled Backup (0 to 23) |
| days | String | Y | Date value for Scheduled Backup |
| month | String | Y | Month value for Scheduled Backup. Leave it as \* since currently it only support Daily and Weekly. |
| weeks | String | Y | Day value for Scheduled Backup. Used when it's configured as Weekly Backup. (mon tue wed thu fri sat sun, default value is ?) |
| userId | String | Y | UserId of the user who modify the configuration |

Triggers a manual (on-demand) system backup.

- Pre-backup validation runs **synchronously**; the actual backup runs **asynchronously** after the response is returned.
    
- HTTP status is **always** **`200 OK`** — success, pre-checks, and validation failures are all signaled via the `Response.code` field in the body.
    
- The posted `path` must equal the value currently stored in `sysbackup.conf` (normalized compare) — arbitrary-path backups are blocked.
    

Use `GET /api/v2/setting/getBackupDbConfig` to retrieve the current backup config and echo its `path` value back in the request body.

---

## Request Headers

| Header | Required | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `bs-session-id` | yes | Resolved to user identity for audit logging |
| `Content-Type` | yes | `application/json` |

---

## Request Body

JSON `Backup` object. **Only** **`path`** **is consumed by this endpoint.** All other fields (`use`, `cycle`, `minute`, `hour`, `days`, `month`, `weeks`, etc.) are accepted for schema symmetry with the scheduled-backup config but are **ignored**.

`path` is deployment-specific — it must equal the value in `{BIOSTAR_HOME}\sysbackup.conf` after `Path.normalize()`. The example value below is illustrative; always read the current backup config first.

``` json
{
  "path": "<value from sysbackup.conf, e.g. C:\\BioStar\\backup>"
}

```

If `sysbackup.conf` is missing or unreadable, every call fails with `code: "2017"`.

---

## Response Envelope

All responses use the `BackupResponse` wrapper. The `Backup` block is **always all-null** on this endpoint — only the `Response` block carries meaningful data.

``` json
{
  "Backup": {
    "userId": null, "use": null, "cycle": null, "day": null,
    "weekday": null, "time": null, "path": null, "overwritable": null,
    "minute": null, "hour": null, "days": null, "month": null,
    "weeks": null, "lastBackup": null, "biostarVersion": null
  },
  "Response": {
    "code": "<string>",
    "link": "https://support.supremainc.com/en/support/home",
    "message": "<string>",
    "by": "web"
  }
}

```

---

## Response Codes

| `Response.code` | HTTP Status | Trigger | `Response.message` |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `0` | 200 | Validation passed; backup started asynchronously | `success` |
| `2017` | 200 | A backup is already running, or posted `path` does not match configured backup path | `Backup is already running` / `Only local server path can be configured` |
| `71000` | 200 | `BACKUP_VALIDATION_FAILED` — one or more pre-backup checks failed | `;`\-joined, distinct i18n message keys (see Validation Message Keys below) |

### Success example

``` json
{
  "Backup": { "...": "all null" },
  "Response": {
    "code": "0",
    "link": "https://support.supremainc.com/en/support/home",
    "message": "success",
    "by": "web"
  }
}

```

### Validation failure example (`code: "71000"`)

``` json
{
  "Backup": { "...": "all null" },
  "Response": {
    "code": "71000",
    "link": "https://support.supremainc.com/en/support/home",
    "message": "setting.backup.validation.encryptionKeyNotFound; setting.backup.validation.backupNotWritable",
    "by": "web"
  }
}

```

---

## Pre-backup Validation Checks

`BackupValidationServiceImpl.validateBeforeBackup()` runs the following checks in order. All checks are collected (does **not** short-circuit); failures are joined into `Response.message`.

| # | Check | Inputs |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Install location (`BIOSTAR_HOME`) resolvable | `ConfigUtils.getHomePath()` |
| 2a | `enc_key_dir` is not a filesystem root | `system.conf → BioStar.enc_key_dir` |
| 2b | `enckey` file exists and is readable | `{enc_key_dir}/enckey` |
| 2c | Required companion files exist and are readable | `{enc_key_dir}/` |
| 3 | `{BIOSTAR_HOME}/util` exists and is readable | `BIOSTAR_HOME` |
| 4 | Image-log directory exists and is readable | `system.conf → img_log_dir` (defaults to `{BIOSTAR_HOME}/imagelog`) |
| 5a | Backup path configured in `sysbackup.conf` | Backup config |
| 5b | Backup directory exists | `sysbackup.conf → path` |
| 5c | Backup directory is writable (temp-file probe) | `sysbackup.conf → path` |
| 6 | Server IP configured (`server_addr` or `ssl_addr`) | `system.conf` / `setting.conf` |
| 7 | Service-token dir resolvable, exists, readable; `service_token` file present and readable | `system-device-server.conf` |
| 8 | `{BIOSTAR_HOME}/nginx/conf` exists and is readable | filesystem |
| 9 | `{BIOSTAR_HOME}/nginx/html/upload` exists and is readable | filesystem |
| 10 | `{BIOSTAR_HOME}/cert` exists and is readable | filesystem |

---

## Validation Message Keys (`code: 71000`)

`Response.message` is a `"; "`\-separated list of distinct i18n keys, one per failed check.

| messageKey | Cause |
| --- | --- |
| `setting.backup.validation.configurationCouldNotLoaded` | `system.conf` / `setting.conf` could not be loaded or parsed |
| `setting.backup.validation.locationNotfound` | `BIOSTAR_HOME` not set |
| `setting.backup.validation.encryptionKeyDirIsDriveRoot` | `enc_key_dir` resolves to a filesystem root (e.g. `C:\`) |
| `setting.backup.validation.encryptionKeyNotFound` | `enckey` or required companion file missing |
| `setting.backup.validation.encryptionKeyNotReadable` | `enckey` or required companion file not readable |
| `setting.backup.validation.utilDirNotFound` | `{BIOSTAR_HOME}/util` missing |
| `setting.backup.validation.utilDirNotReadable` | `{BIOSTAR_HOME}/util` not readable |
| `setting.backup.validation.imageLogNotFound` | Image-log directory missing |
| `setting.backup.validation.imageLogNotReadable` | Image-log directory not readable |
| `setting.backup.validation.backupNotAvailable` | `sysbackup.conf` not available |
| `setting.backup.validation.backupSysbackConf` | Backup path blank in `sysbackup.conf` |
| `setting.backup.validation.backupNotFound` | Configured backup directory does not exist |
| `setting.backup.validation.backupNotWritable` | Backup directory exists but is not writable |
| `setting.backup.validation.serverIpNotConfigured` | Neither `system.conf:server_addr` nor `setting.conf:https.ssl_addr` is set |
| `setting.backup.validation.serviceToken` | Install location unavailable when resolving service-token dir |
| `setting.backup.validation.deviceServerConfiguration` | `system-device-server.conf` missing |
| `setting.backup.validation.parseDeviceServer` | `system-device-server.conf` failed to parse as JSON |
| `setting.backup.validation.systemDeviceServerConf` | `BioStar.service_token_dir` not set in `system-device-server.conf` |
| `setting.backup.validation.serviceTokenMissing` | Service-token directory or `service_token` file missing/unreadable |
| `setting.backup.validation.fileFolderRequired` | `{install}/nginx/conf`, `{install}/nginx/html/upload`, or `{install}/cert` missing/unreadable |

---

## Side Effects on Validation Failure

- Error logged: `logger.error(validationResult.getDetailedLog())`
    
- Audit log entry written: `Backup Failed` (attributed to the calling user and remote address)
    
- Backup-failed event fired: `backupAlertService.sendBackupFailedEventOnly()`
    

Note: `remoteAddress` is resolved via `remoteAccessService.resolveClientIpAddress(request)`, so Remote Access (ngrok-tunneled) callers are correctly attributed.

#### Changes:

- v1.0.2:
    
    - Async backup behavior and synchronous pre-validation
        
    - HTTP-always-200 with `Response.code` as the real signal
        
    - `path` constraint (must match `sysbackup.conf` after normalization)
        
    - Response envelope structure (Backup block always null, Response block carries data)
        
    - Response codes table (`0`, `2017`, `71000`) with success and failure examples
        
    - Full pre-backup validation checks table (10 check categories)
        
    - All 20 validation message keys with causes
        
    - Side effects on failure (error log, audit log, backup-failed event)

This API is used to add a card to BioStar X. #### Parameters: | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | card_type:id | Number | Y | Type card id of the card. See "View All Card Types" for all type of cards.
0: CSN card
1: Wiegand Card
2: Secure Credential Card (Smart card)
3: Access on Card (Smart card)
4: Mobile CSN Card
5: Wiegand Mobile
6: QR/Barcode
7: BioStar X QR | | card_type:type | Number | Y | Type allocation of the card type. View the list using `/api/cards/types` to determine the correct value. | | card_id | Number | Y | Input value shown/read when scanning the card. | #### **NOTE:** - This API will return error if the `card_id` is already exist on the database. Use "Check Card Availability" API to validate. - This API will return an error when generating CSN Card with invalid card id. `Error Code: ACB_ERROR_CODE.415` Cases of invalid card id: - CSN with card id staring with 0 - CSN with card id using letters - More than 32 characters

This API is used to add a card to BioStar X. #### Parameters: | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | card_type:id | Number | Y | Type card id of the card. See "View All Card Types" for all type of cards.
0: CSN card
1: Wiegand Card
2: Secure Credential Card (Smart card)
3: Access on Card (Smart card)
4: Mobile CSN Card
5: Wiegand Mobile
6: QR/Barcode
7: BioStar X QR | | card_type:type | Number | Y | Type allocation of the card type. View the list using /api/cards/types to determine the correct value. | | card_id | Number | Y | Input value recorded on the card. | | display_card_id | Number | N | Input value shown/read when scanning the card. | | isUserPhoto | Boolean | Y | Toggle true if the credential will be using the user photo (photo must exist) | | isDepartment | Boolean | Y | Toggle true if the credential will be using the user department (department field must be filled) | | isTitle | Boolean | Y | Toggle true if the credential will be using the user title (title field must be filled). | | start_datetime | Date | Y | Start datetime for the credential availability. | | expiry_datetime | Date | Y | End datetime for the credential availability. | #### NOTE: - This API will return error if the card_id is already exist on the database. Use "Check Card Availability" API to validate.

For this example, there's no conversion required since the card id already have 35 bit value. 19044429267 > Decimal 100 0110 1111 0010 0010 1110 1101 1101 0011 > Binary (35 Bits) Just make sure to use format id which doesn't use facility code. #### Parameters: | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | card_type:id | Number | Y | Type card id of the card. See "View All Card Types" for all type of cards.
0: CSN card
1: Wiegand Card
2: Secure Credential Card (Smart card)
3: Access on Card (Smart card)
4: Mobile CSN Card
5: Wiegand Mobile
6: QR/Barcode
7: BioStar X QR | | card_type:type | Number | Y | Type allocation of the card type. View the list using /api/cards/types to determine the correct value. | | card_id | Number | Y | Input value shown/read when scanning the card. | | wiegand_format_id:id | Number | Y | If card_type is wiegand, then fill this parameter:
0: 26 bit format
1: HID 37 bit-H10302
2: HID 37 bit-H10304
3: HID Corporate 1000 (35 bit)
4: HID Corporate 1000 (48 bit)
5\~14: Custom formats. | #### NOTE: - This API will return error if the `card_id` is already exist on the database. Use "Check Card Availability" API to validate.

To input card that is using facility code, there's conversion that needs to be done first.

The process is:

1.  Get the Facility Code and Card ID
2.  Convert it to Binary
3.  Combine the binary value of the Facility Code and Card ID
4.  Convert it to Decimal
5.  Use the converted value on card_id parameter
6.  Don't forget to select the corresponding wiegand_format_id
    

* * *

**Conversion when The Card is Using Facility Code**

* * *

> E: Even  
> O: Odd  
> C: Facility Code  
> D: ID

**26 bit SIA Standard-H10301**  
Position: 0-25

| Field | E | CCCCCCCC | DDDDDDDDDDDDDDDD | O |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Position | 0 | 1-8 | 9-24 | 25 |

E&O : Not included on the calculation  
Bit : 8 bits + 16 bits  
Byte: 1 byte + 2 bytes  
Hexa: FF + FFFF

Example:  
Facility Code + Card ID  
20 + 10000 > Decimal  
14 + 2710 > Hex  
14 2710 > Combined Hex (CH)  
1320720 > Converted CH to Decimal. Use this as input

**HID 37 bit-H10304**  
Position: 0-36

| Field | E | CCCCCCCCCCCCCCCC | DDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDD | O |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Position | 0 | 1-16 | 17-35 | 36 |

E&O : Not included on the calculation  
Bit : 16 bits + 19 bits

Example:  
Facility Code + Card ID  
43962 + 123456 > Decimal  
1010 1011 1011 1010 + 001 1110 0010 0100 0000 > Binary (16 + 19 bits)  
1010 1011 1011 1010 0011 1100 0100 1000 000 > Combined Binary (CB)  
23048872512 > Converted CB to Decimal. Use this as input

**HID Corporate 1000**  
Position 0-34

| Field | O | E | CCCCCCCCCCCCCCCC | DDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDD | O |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Position | 0 | 1 | 2-13 | 14-33 | 34 |

E&O : Not included on the calculation  
Bit : 12 bits + 20 bits

Example:  
Facility Code + Card ID  
2543 + 1005255 > Decimal  
1001 1110 1111 + 1111 0101 0110 1100 0111 > Binary (12 + 20 bits)  
1001 1110 1111 1111 0101 0110 1100 0111 > Combined Binary (CB)  
2667534023 > Converted CB to Decimal. Use this as input

**HID Corporate 1000 (48bit)**  
Position 0-47

| Field | O | E | CCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCCC | DDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDDD | O |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Position | 0 | 1 | 2-23 | 24-46 | 47 |

E&O : Not included on the calculation  
Bit : 22 bits + 23 bits

Example:  
Facility Code + Card ID  
2813689 + 4336695  
10 1010 1110 1110 1111 1001 + 100 0010 0010 1100 0011 0111 > Binary  
1010 1011 1011 1011 1110 0110 0001 0001 0110 0001 1011 1 > Combined Binary (CB)  
23602938391607 > Converted CB to Decimal. Use this as input

item.name: User

request.description:
This API returns the next available user ID based on the currently assigned user IDs. If user IDs are numeric, the response will be the next sequential number. If alphanumeric user IDs are enabled, the API will only consider numeric user IDs and return the next available numeric value.

- If the currently used user IDs are 1, 2, 3, the API response will be 4.
    
- If user ID type is configured to **alphanumeric**, but existing IDs are 1, 2, 3, x4, 4x, the response will still be 4 (since "x4" and "4x" are ignored).
    

#### Notes:

- The API automatically filters out non-numeric user IDs when alphanumeric mode is enabled.
    
- This does not create a new user ID, only suggests the next available one.
    

#### Response Properties:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| User.user_id | String | The next available user ID. |

This API is used to create a new user. #### BODY Parameters: | **Parameters** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | user_id | String | Y | ID of the User. Unique.

When User ID Type in Setting > Server is set to Number, a number between 1 and 4294967295 can be entered. When Alphanumeric is set, a combination of alphabetic characters and numbers can be entered.
Do not use spaces when entering ID.
Numbers or Alphanumeric characters can be set for user_id. For more details, refer to Setting Section of this documentation. | | name | String | N | Name of the user. Up to 48 characters. | | user_group_id | Number | Y | ID of the User Group that the user will be under. Refer to User Group Section of this documentation for listing available User Groups. | | disabled | Boolean | N | Toggle User active or not; false for active, true for non-active. | | start_datetime | Date/Time | Y | Start active period for the User.
The earliest allowable value is 2001-01-01 00:00. | | expiry_datetime | Date/Time | Y | End active period for the User.
Value can not exceed 2037-12-31 23:59. | | email | String | N | Email address of the User. | | department | String | N | Department of the User. Up to 64 characters. | | user_title | String | N | Title of the User. | | photo | Base64 | N | Photo of the User. | | phone | String | N | Phone number of the User. | | permission | Number | N | Operator Level of the User.

Use **View User by Account Level** **(GET /api/permissions)** to view the list of available Operator Level and its ID).

**None**: The user has no operator privilege. To use this Operator Level, remove permission parameter from the API Request.
**Administrator**: The user can use all menus.
**User Operator**: The user can only use the USER and PREFERENCE menus.
**Monitoring Operator**: The user can use the MONITORING and PREFERENCE menus and only view the DASHBOARD, USER, DEVICE, DOOR, ZONE and ACCESS CONTROL menus.
**Video Operator**: The user can only use the VIDEO menu.
**T&A Operator**: The user can only use the TIME ATTENDANCE menu and only view the USER menu.
**User**: The user can only view own information and T&A records. | | access_groups | Number | N | ID of the Access Group that the User will have access. Refer to Access Group Section of this documentation for listing available Access Groups.
Can enter more than 1 Access Group. | | login_id | String | N | Required when permission parameter is not removed. | | password | String | N | Required when permission parameter is not removed. | | user_ip | String | N | Limit the access of the User so he/she can only login from the registered IP Address.

The user IP can be entered in the format xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx. Each octet can only be entered in numbers between 0 and 255.
Users whose user IP is not registered can log in from any IP. | | pin | Number | N | PIN of the user. Maximum 32 digit. | When `Setting` > `Server` is set to Number, you can make use of endpoint `/api/users/next_user_id` to automatically get the next `user_id`. Refer to `GET` Find Next Available UserID for the detail of this endpoint. #### Response Properties: | **Parameter** | Type | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | total | String | Total of created user | | user_id | String | Created user's ID | | name | String | Created user's name | #### Known errors: - name is more than 48 characters - Using single quote on name - Duplicate user_id, email, or login_id - Empty user_id, user_group_id, start_datetime, expiry_datetime - Using user_group_id:id which doesn't exist - Putting "/" at the end of the endpoint - Password with 3 or more subsequent letter or number

This API is used to create multiple users at once.

Things to be noted when using this API:

- Parameters which can be used is the same as when creating a user (see `Create New User`)
    
- `user_id` should be unique.
    
- To add a user credential using this API, you must create the credential and include the credential ID in the body.
    
- Because syncing users to their devices takes time, it is recommended to add less than 30 users at a time.

Up to 64 characters. | | user_title | String | N | Title of the User. | | photo | Base64 | N | Photo of the User. | | phone | String | N | Phone number of the User. | | permission | Number | N | Operator Level of the User.

Use **View User by Account Level** **(GET /api/permissions)** to view the list of available Operator Level and its ID).

**None**: The user has no operator privilege. To use this Operator Level, remove permission parameter from the API Request.
**Administrator**: The user can use all menus.
**User Operator**: The user can only use the USER and PREFERENCE menus.
**Monitoring Operator**: The user can use the MONITORING and PREFERENCE menus and only view the DASHBOARD, USER, DEVICE, DOOR, ZONE and ACCESS CONTROL menus.
**Video Operator**: The user can only use the VIDEO menu.

**User**: The user can only view own information and T&A records. | | access_groups | Number | N | ID of the Access Group that the User will have access. Refer to Access Group Section of this documentation for listing available Access Groups.
Can enter more than 1 Access Group. | | login_id | String | N | Required when permission parameter is not removed. | | password | String | N | Required when permission parameter is not removed. | | user_ip | String | N | Limit the access of the User so he/she can only login from the registered IP Address.

Maximum 32 digit. | | isExludeSyncADUser | Number | N | 1: User will be excluded from AD or EntraID sync. | When **Setting > Server** is set to **Number**, you can make use of endpoint `/api/users/next_user_id` to automatically get the next user_id. Refer to `GET Find Next Available UserID` for the detail of this endpoint. #### Known errors: - name is more than 48 characters - Using single quote on name - Duplicate user_id, email, or login_id - Empty user_id, user_group_id, start_datetime, expiry_datetime - Using user_group_id:id which doesn't exist - Putting "/" at the end of the endpoint - Password with 3 or more subsequent letter or number - Start and expiry date difference is more than 30 years

This API is used for updating user information in bulk.

The difference of this API and `/api/users?id=1+2+3` is that this API can update different data of many users at the same time.

**Warning:** The maximum number of users that can be modified in a single API call is **100**.

#### BODY Parameters:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| user_id | String | Y | User ID of the user. This will be used as the update target. |

All user information parameters in `/api/users` are included.

#### Response Example:

``` json
{
    "UserCollection": {
        "rows": [
            {
                "user_id": "3",
                "ret_code": "0"
            },
            {
                "user_id": "2",
                "ret_code": "224"
            },
            {
                "user_id": "1",
                "ret_code": "0"
            }
        ]
    },
    "Response": {
        "code": "1",
        "link": "https://support.supremainc.com/en/support/home",
        "message": "Request is successful, but partially"
    }
}

```

#### **Response Code:**

**ret_code** presents BioStar X response code for modifying each user. The meaning on each code is explained on `[BioStar X Install Path]\nginx\html\resources\messages_en`.

For example `ret_code 224`, find the number in the properties file: `ACB_ERROR_CODE.224 = Cannot use the same previous PW`.

"Response.code" could be 0, 1, 8.

- 0 : Overall success.
    
- 1 : Partial success
    
- 8 : Overall failed.
    

##### **HTTP Response Code:**

- `200 OK` : Overall success, partial success or overall failed.
    
- `500 Internal Server Error`: An unexpected error occurred on the server.

item.name: User Credential: Misc (Include Device Default Authentication Mode)

request.description:
On this example you can see how to use stated parameters to adjust misc. credential values.

#### Parameters on this example:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| pin | Number | N | PIN of the User |
| security_level | Number | N | To set the security level of the credential. The higher the security level is set, the false rejection rate (FRR) gets higher, but the false acceptance rate (FAR) gets lower. Acceptable value: 0 to 5 |
| private_operation_modes |  |  |  |
| :operation_method | Number |  | The first credential of an Authentication Mode (0: ID, 1: Card, 2: Fingerprint/Biometric (Standard), 3: Face) |
| :operation_mode | Number |  | Authentication Mode combination (see below for reference, "-1" = do not use) |
| :exclude | Boolean |  | If Include Device Default Authentication Mode option is on, so when you want to also follow the Auth Modes that the devices have, “private_operation_modes” will have same number of elements that you actually configured. But if Exclude Device Default Authentication Mode is on, you need to add four or more elements because all the values of “operation_method” should be included to override device’s Auth Modes. |

item.name: User Credential: Misc (Exclude Device Default Authentication Mode)

request.description:
As stated on previous example (User Credential: Misc (Include Device Default Authentication Mode)), when you want to use Exclude Device Default Authentication Mode then you need to state all values of the Authentication Method.

On this example, the Authentication Mode that's being used is Biometric (operation_method: 2) only (operation_mode: 0) and the other Authentication Method: ID (0), Card (1), and Face (3) are set as do not use (operation_mode: -1) and hidden (exclude: true)

To use Extended Auth Mode simply use code that starts from 11 (refer to Authentication Mode Combination Table on User Credential: Misc (Include Device Default Authentication Mode)), do note that this mode only supported by FaceStation F2.

This example shows how to assign a card to a User, which is applicable to CSN, Wiegand, Smart, Mobile, and QR/Barcode.

First the card must be enrolled to the database by using `POST /api/cards`.

After enrolled, use `GET /api/cards` to view all Cards or `GET /api/cards/unassigned` to view all Cards which are unassigned.

Select a card from the response and apply it on the body as shown on the example to assign it to the selected user.

This example shows how to update fingerprint data on a User.

#### BODY Parameters:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| fingerprint_templates |  |  |  |
| :finger_mask | Boolean | N | Set to True to mark the fingerprint as a duress fingerprint. When threatened by someone to open the door, the user can authenticate using this fingerprint to send an alarm signal to BioStar X. |
| :template0 | Raw | Y | First scan template of the fingerprint |
| :template1 | Raw | Y | Second scan template of the fingerprint |
| :isNew | Boolean | N |  |

Use `POST` `/api/devices/:dev_id/scan_fingerprint` to enroll fingerprint  
Use `POST` `/api/server_matching/identify_finger` to check whether the fingerprint already recorded or not.

- Raw data for template0 and template1 available after enrolling a fingerprint.
    
- Fingerprint(s) used for regular access should not be registered as duress fingerprint.
    
- If the fingerprint authentication rate is low, delete the existing fingerprint information and add a new fingerprint.
    
- Use an adequate security level. If 1:1 Security Level is too high, the fingerprint authentication rate may be too low or the false rejection rate (FRR) may be too high.
    
- For best fingerprint scanning quality, make sure to cover the entire surface of the fingerprint sensor with the finger.

This example shows how to add a Face Credential on a User.

You can add the user's faces if the device supports face authentication.

- When registering a face, maintain a distance of 40cm to 80cm between the device and the face.
    
- Be careful not to change the facial expression. (smiling face, drawn face, wink, etc.)
    
- If you do not follow the instructions on the screen, the face registration may take longer or may fail.
    
- Be careful not to cover the eyes or eyebrows.
    
- Do not wear hats, masks, sunglasses or eyeglasses.
    
- Be careful not to display two faces on the screen. Register one person at a time.
    
- It is recommended for a user wearing glasses to register both faces with and without glasses.
    

#### Parameters on this example:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| faces | Array | Y | Main container of the credential |
| :raw_image | Raw | Y | Raw image of the face scan |
| :templates | Array | Y | Container of the face templates |
| ::template | Raw | Y | Face template of the User |

To get data for parameter raw_image and template, use `GET` `/api/devices/:device_id/credentials/face`

Use `POST` `/api/v2/devices/search` to list all device which can support the selected credentials. See API documentation for details on the usage of the API.

When updating a User, it is required to put the credential data as acquired from `GET` `/api/users/:id`  
This is done so the data won't be lost after update.

To delete a Face, skip the parameters on using this API.

You can use `raw_image` value as value on photo parameter of a User.

Visual Face is a credential that captures the user's face with a visual camera. It is different from face information captured with an infrared camera and is only available on devices that support Visual Face. There are two ways to input a Visual Face: by scanning through a enrollment device, or by uploading an image. Up to two (2) visual faces can be added. Use `GET /api/devices/:device_id/credentials/face` to scan Visual Face using an enrollment device. You can enroll a visual face using FaceStation F2. Use `POST /api/v2/devices/search` to list compatible devices. To upload image as a Visual Face: image file size is up to 10MB and supported file formats are JPG, JPEG and PNG. Use `PUT /api/users/check/upload_picture` to check whether the image is suitable for use or not. BODY example on using Upload Picture: ``` json { "User": { "credentials": { "visualFaces": [{ "template_ex_picture": "/9j/4AAQSkZJRgABAQAAAQABAAD/ Truncated" }] }} } ``` Parameters on this example: | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | visualFaces | Array | Y | Main container of Visual Face credential | | :template_ex_normalized_image | Base64 | Y | Cropped image ready for visual face extraction. | | :templates | Array | Y | Container of the templates | | ::credential_bin_type | Number | Y | UNKNOWN = -1
FACE_TEMPLATE = 0
FACE_TEMPLATE_IMAGE = 1
FACE_RAW_IMAGE = 2
FACE_TEMPLATE_EX_VER_2 = 5 (For FaceStation F2)
FACE_TEMPLATE_EX_VER_3 = 9 (For BioStation 3 and W3)
FACE_TEMPLATE_EX_NORMALIZED = 7
FACE_TEMPLATE_EX_PICTURE = 8 | | ::template_ex | Raw | Y | Visual Face template data which includes FaceStation F2 and the new BioStation 3. | | :template_ex_picture | Base64 | Y | Parameter for Upload Picture raw data |

This API is used to check whether the image that will be uploaded on Visual Face credential is according to specification or not.

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| template_ex_picture | Base64 | Y | Image that will be checked |

- Supported image file size is up to 10MB.
- Supported image file formats are JPG, JPEG and PNG.

request.description:
This example shows a workaround using this API to get multiple users data by adjusting the parameters.

The advantage of this workaround is that the API response can be adjusted to only show only the required users directly.

*   Query parameter search_month value is set to 0 or removed
*   Body parameter conditions:column value set to user_id, :operator value to 2 (contains), and :value can specify multiple user id which uses comma as separator.
    

For BODY Parameter details refer to POST List Idle Users.

query.description: Use this to enable Advanced Search query result.

request.description:
By adding `adv=mode1` on Query Parameter, Batch Edit feature can works with Advanced Search result.

The API works by query users based on adv_criteria object, then apply new value based on input in User object.

#### BODY Parameters:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| User | Object | Y | User data to change, existing payload |
| adv_criteria | Object | Y | Advanced Search criteria, this is required for searching user data that want to be changed |
| adv_criteria.limit | Number | Y | Advanced Search search result limit |
| adv_criteria.order_by | String | Y | Advanced Search search result order |
| adv_criteria.user_group_id | Number | N | User Group ID to search, this is specifically for when clicking leftmost user group and do Advanced Search |
| adv_criteria.user_group_ids | Array | N | List of User Group IDs to search, this field value is from Advanced Search's search form |
| adv_criteria.user_id | String | N | User ID to search |
| adv_criteria.user_email | String | N | Email to search |
| adv_criteria.user_access_group_ids | String | N | List of access groups to search (format : 17,18) |
| adv_criteria.user_operator_level_id | Number | Y | Operator Level ID to search. Value : 0 = user with all operator level, -1 = user with no operator level, else ID of operator level |
| adv_criteria.user_department | String | N | Department of user to search |
| adv_criteria.user_title | String | N | Title of user to search |
| adv_criteria.user_card | String | N | User card ID to search |
| adv_criteria.user_custom_field_1 until adv_criteria.user_custom_field_10 | String | N | User custom fields to search |
| adv_criteria.user_status | String | N | User status to search. false = Active, true = Inactive |

#### Response Example:

``` json
{
    "FailedUserCollection": {
        "total": "0",
        "rows": ""
    },
    "Response": {
        "code": "0",
        "link": "https:\/\/support.supremainc.com\/en\/support\/home",
        "message": "Success"
    }
}

```

#### Response Properties:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| FailedUserCollection | Object | List of failed user to edit |
| Response | Object | Response from backend |
| Response.code | String | Indicate that the request is failed or not (0 is success, other is failed) |
| Response.link | String | Suprema link |
| Response.message | String | Success or failed message from backend |

request.description:
This API is used to import User Data from a .csv file to the BioStar 2 database. To run this API successfully, it is imperative to run POST /api/attachments first to upload the .csv file to the server install path. After .csv file got uploaded to the server, put the filename value from the above API to the uri and fileName parameter on this API. BODY parameters on this API: | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | File:uri | String | Y | Path of the .csv file | | File:fileName | String | Y | File Name of the .csv file | | CsvOption | | Y | Container for available importing options | | :start_line | Number | Y | Specify row number to start data import | | :import_option | Number | Y | Options when same ID exist in BioStar 2: 1=Preserve Data, 2=Overwrite Data | | Query:headers | Array | Y | List of fields on .csv file. Number of fields should match with system. Use GET /api/users/csv_option to see system fields.
Use GET /download/:csv_filename to see the content of the intended .csv file. | | Query:columns | Array | Y | List of fields on the BioStar 2. Use GET /api/users/csv_option to get this list. | Fields listed on Query parameter are also used for field mapping between .csv file and BioStar 2 database. For example field user_id on Query:headers (.csv) will be matched with the first field of Query:columns (BioStar 2). To change this mapping, adjust the sequence on Query:headers as desired (eg. exchange field department with user_id so the value of department will be used on user_id on BioStar 2). To skip a field from mapping, put empty string ("") on the intended field. If there's no issue on the import process, the API will shows "Success" response. If there's issue (duplicate email, etc.), the response will show that the "Request is successful but partially" and will include a filename which contain records that caused the errors which can be downloaded by using GET /download/:csv_filename If the CSV file to import contains data for custom user fields and the fields do not exist on the server, then the data for the fields will be ignored during the import process. To enter user information in a language other than English or Korean, save the CSV file in UTF-8 format.

request.description:
You can store the data file on external storage (USB) and import to BioStar 2 or device. Up to 500,000 users can be moved from server to device or from device to device.

- The exported data file from devices using older firmware version cannot be imported into BioStar 2. Make sure always use the latest version of firmware.
    
- If you use BioStar 2.8.10 or later, data exported from the previous version of BioStar 2 cannot be imported.
    
- If you use BioStar 2.8.10 or later, it is impossible to read data from a device that are using older firmware version. Upgrade the device’s firmware to a compatible version. Compatible devices and firmware versions are as follows:
    
    - BioStation 2 FW 1.9.0 or later > device_type: 8
        
    - BioStation A2 FW 1.8.0 or later > device_type: 9
        
    - FaceStation 2 FW 1.4.0 or later > device_type: 10
        
    - FaceStation F2 FW 1.0.0 or later > device_type: 29
        
    - X-Station 2 FW 1.0.0 or later > device_type: 32
        
- If the fingerprint template format is different, the data file cannot be imported. For example, the data file exported from a device which uses the Suprema fingerprint template format cannot be imported into a device which uses the ISO fingerprint template format.
    
- When importing user data with visual faces enrolled from FaceStation F2, the existing data will be retained if there are already visual face data enrolled on BioStar 2 via Upload Image or a mobile device.
    

#### BODY Parameters:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| device_type | Number | Y | Device type which the exported data will be applied to |
| group_id | Number | Y | Group ID of the device. Default value=1 |
| adv_criteria | Object | Y | See Batch Edit with Advanced Search for adv_criteria details. |

- The exported data file includes the profile photo, user ID, name, period, access group, PIN, auth. mode, credentials (face, fingerprint, card, mobile access card, visual face, BioStar 2 QR, QR/Barcode), 1:1 security level.
    
- You can export visual face data directly registered from the device only. It is not possible to export the data registered with the other methods, such as image file upload, CSV import, and mobile devices.
    
- Be sure that the device is selected correctly. Otherwise, the device cannot recognize the data file.
    

#### Response Example:

``` json
{
  "File" : {
    "uri" : "BioStar2_20241030_093821.tgz"
  },
  "Response" : {
    "code" : "0",
    "link" : "https://support.supremainc.com/en/support/home",
    "message" : "Success"
  }
}

```

#### Response Properties:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| File | Object | File object |
| File.uri | String | Exported file name |
| Response | Object | Response object |
| Response.code | Number | Indicates success or failure |
| Response.link | String | Suprema link |
| Response.message | String | Success or error message |

#### Changes:

- v2.9.8:
    
    - Added `adv` Query Parameter for enabling Advanced Search query capability.
        
    - Added `adv_criteria` on Body Parameter as example on how to use Advanced Search query.

*   The exported data file from devices using older firmware version cannot be imported into BioStar 2. Make sure always use the latest version of firmware.
*   If you use BioStar 2.8.10 or later, data exported from the previous version of BioStar 2 cannot be imported.
*   If you use BioStar 2.8.10 or later, it is impossible to read data from a device that are using older firmware version. Upgrade the device’s firmware to a compatible version. Compatible devices and firmware versions are as follows:

request.description:
This API is used to compare 2 fingerprint scan to determine whether it's the same finger or not.

As shown on the example, it is applied on `template0` and `template1` where `template0` is the 1st scan of the finger and `template1` is the 2nd scan.

To get the value for these parameters, use `POST /api/devices/:dev_id/scan_fingerprint` two times. The first scan for `template0` and second scan for `template1`.

It will return HTTP 200 and shows message result: true on success, HTTP 500 and shows message result: false on failure.

#### BODY Parameters:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| template0 | Base64 | Y | 1st scan template of the fingerprint |
| template1 | Base64 | Y | 2nd scan template of the fingerprint |
| security_level | Number | N | Set the verification security level. Higher number will have stricter requirement. |

This API is used to verify duplicate fingerprint.

If fingerprint already exist, it will return success (HTTP 200) and response will show the user which currently using the fingerprint.

Use the result of `POST /api/devices/:dev_id/scan_fingerprint` for the value of parameter template (either the 1st or 2nd scan).

If fingerprint available, it will return server error (HTTP 500) with response showing message "Failed to identify fingerprint.".

#### BODY Parameters:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| template | Base64 | Y | Template of the fingerprint which will be validated. |

variable.description: ID of the device which will be used to scan fingerprint.

request.description:
This API is used to scan a fingerprint.

On calling this API, it is required to specify the device which will be used as fingerprint scanner. It is done by specifying Device ID on the API Path, which marked by :dev_id.

The procedure to add a fingerprint as follows:  
\- Scan a finger using this API  
\- Use `POST /api/server_matching/identify_finger` to check if it's already exist  
\- If it doesn't exist, then run a second scan using this API for the same finger as the first scan  
\- Compare 1st and 2nd scan result using `POST` `/api/devices/:dev_id/verify_fingerprint` to make sure that 1st and 2nd scan are the same finger  
\- Assign the enrolled finger to a user using `POST|PUT` `/api/users/:id`

#### BODY Parameters:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| enroll_quality | Number | N | Set a fingerprint enrollment quality level. Any fingerprint which does not meet the quality requirement will not be enrolled. |
| raw_image | Boolean | N | Set this option to view the original image when a fingerprint is scanned. |

request.description:
Delete all event logs stored inside one or more devices.

This endpoint instructs selected devices to **remove all event logs stored locally on the device**.

Deleted logs **cannot be recovered** and **cannot be restored from the server**.

Before using this API, ensure that the device event logs have already been successfully uploaded to the server.

Devices that **do not store logs** are automatically excluded from the deletion process:

- Slave devices
    
- Virtual devices
    
- Wiegand devices
    

#### Permissions

This endpoint requires **Administrator privileges**.

#### Behavior

**Case 1: Clear logs for specific devices**

If **id** contains one or more device IDs, the endpoint clears logs only for those devices.

Example

```
DELETE /api/devices/clear_log?id=1001 1002 1003
OR
DELETE /api/devices/clear_log?id=1001+1002+1003

```

---

**Case 2: Clear logs for all devices**

If **id=**\*, the endpoint targets all devices.

Example

```
DELETE /api/devices/clear_log?id=*

```

---

**Case 3: Clear logs for filtered devices**

If **id=**\*, optional filters may be used to restrict the target set.

Example

```
DELETE /api/devices/clear_log?id=*&group_id=10&offset=0&limit=50

```

This means:

- target all devices
    
- restrict to group `10`
    
- apply paging with `offset=0` and `limit=50`
    

#### Notes

- This API deletes **only the event logs stored in the device**.
    
- Event logs already stored on the **server are not affected**.
    
- Deleted logs **cannot be recovered**.
    
- Devices that do not store logs (Slave, Virtual, Wiegand) are automatically excluded.

request.description: This API is used to delete Door(s).

This API is used to view various door status information such as the door status, relay status, alarm and last event.

*   Fire alarm unlocked: A state where the door designated as a fire alarm zone is unlocked because a fire has broken out.
*   Manual Lock: A state where the door is locked because the administrator has locked it manually.
*   Manual Unlock: A state where the door is unlocked and able to enter without an authentication because the administrator has unlocked it manually.
*   Schedule Locked: A state where the door is locked by the schedule that has been set.
*   Schedule Unlocked: A state where the door is unlocked by the schedule that has been set.
*   Normal: A state where a user can enter the door after an authentication.

#### Response Example:

``` json
{
  "DeviceResponse": {
    "rows": [
      {
        "id": "50335900",
        "code": "0"
      }
    ],
    "result": "true"
  },
  "Response": {
    "code": "0",
    "link": "https://support.supremainc.com/en/support/home",
    "message": "Success"
  }
}

```

#### Response Properties:

```
- DeviceResponse : The response from device.
    - result : success/error.
    - rows : affected devices with success/error code
- Response : the response from web server, each with the following properties:
    - code : success/error code.
    - link : Suprema support link.
    - message : Success/error message.

```

#### Errors:

This API uses the following error codes:

- `400 Bad Request`: The request was malformed or missing required parameters.
    
- `401 Unauthorized`: The API key provided was invalid or missing.
    
- `404 Not Found`: The requested resource was not found.
    
- `500 Internal Server Error`: An unexpected error occurred on the server.

This API is used to create elevator.

The minimum values is name, elevator_group_id(id, name), reader_devices(id, name), control_device_id(id, name, device_type_id), floors(name, order, elevator_device_id, relay_index). The rest can be removed.

##### Controller - `control_device_id`

Select a device that controls the elevator access permission.

- Only a master device can be selected.
    
- You can select it from the list of registered devices.
    

##### Reader - `reader_devices`

Select a device you intend to use for authentication.

- You can select a device among the master device, slave device, and Wiegand device.
    
- You can select up to 4 readers.
    
- OM-120 cannot be set as the reader.
    

##### Module - `module_devices`

Select OM-120 to control the elevator button relay.

- Only OM-120 can be selected.
    

##### Total Number of Floors - `floors`

Enter the total number of floors that you can move using the elevator.

- Up to 192 floors can be entered.

`To DELETE:` Fill in all Quick Action(s) parameter (including ID). Input all Quick Action(s) besides the one that will be deleted. To delete all, simply send an empty payload.

Install Active Directory Certificate Services and set the keystore password by referring to Active Directory Encryption. | | adlogin | Boolean | Y | Enable logging into BioStar X using the Windows Active Directory account. | | RedirectionUri | String | Y | Required for EntraId interworking. | | adEnable | String | Y | Set to 0. | | adType | String | Y | Interworking target classification.
Active Directory: A
EntraId: E | | prevAdType | String | Y | It is required to enter the previous connection information when modifying the interworking target. If there is no change, enter the current connection information. | | prevDomainName | String | Y | It is required to enter the previous connection information when modifying the interworking target. If there is no change, enter the current connection information. | | prevServerAddr | String | Y | It is required to enter the previous connection information when modifying the interworking target. If there is no change, enter the current connection information. | | resetSync | Boolean | Y | Delete existing users and user groups due to changes in connection information and set them to **true** if new settings are required. default is **false**. | | scheduleSyncInterval | Number | Y | Set the time interval for automatic synchronization.
0: Manual synchronization
30 to 10080: Automatic synchronization | | syncEvtCheck | Boolean | Y | Settings for whether to leave event logs on BioStar X.
**true**: event logs are kept on BioStar X as usual. | | syncType | String | Y | Toggle automatic synchronization.
Manual synchronization: N
Automatic synchronization: A | See these articles for more information on connecting BioStar X with Active Directory: - [https://support.supremainc.com/en/support/solutions/articles/24000044140](https://support.supremainc.com/en/support/solutions/articles/24000044140) - [https://support.supremainc.com/en/support/solutions/articles/24000041940--biostar-2-how-to-confirm-active-directory-information/en](https://support.supremainc.com/en/support/solutions/articles/24000041940--biostar-2-how-to-confirm-active-directory-information/en)

It is required to put domain name information on the body.

The response value is returned in JSON format. At this time, `Collection.rows.userGroups` is used for the next user group update.

#### BODY Parameters:

| --- | --- | --- | --- |

This API is used to set the user field configuration.

The payload is in the form of a JSON array.  
Each array has BioStar X column name (columnm), AD column name (adcolumnm), whether to use (use), and column description (columnmdescription).

> \[ 
  
> {"columnm":"usrid","adcolumnm":"sAMAccountName","use":"true","columnmdescription":"userid"} {"columnm":"nm","adcolumnm":"displayName" ,"use":"true","columnmdescription":"User NAME"} 
  
> \] 
  

If you set the payload as above, you can sync the username as well.

This API is used to synchronizes AD user information with Biostar X.

If there is no user group change or field reorganization then only this synchronization operation is sufficient.

serverAddress, userName, password, userDomainName, and useSSL must be sent in the payload.

lastSyncTime, lastSyncResult, lastUpdateTime fields on response are "recent sync time with ad server", "recent sync result", "recent update time of ad server information",  
It can be checked through `GET /api/v2/setting/adserversetting`

#### BODY Parameters:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| serverAddress | String | Y | IP Address of the Domain Controller |
| userName | String | Y | User Name which will be used to connect to the Domain Controller |
| password | String | Y | User Name's corresponding password |
| userDomainName | String | Y | BaseDN of the Domain Controller |
| useSSL | Boolean | Y | Option to use encryption when communicating with a Windows Domain Controller |
| adType | String | N | AD type |
| RedirectionUri | String | N | Redirection URI for Entra ID authentication |
| scheduleSyncInterval | Integer | N | Interval for scheduled synchronization |
| syncType | String | N | Synchronization type |
| adlogin | Boolean | N | Option to use AD login |
| adEnable | String | N | Option to enable AD |
| syncEvtCheck | Boolean | N | Option to check sync events |
| prevAdType | String | N | Previous AD type |
| prevDomainName | String | N | Previous domain name |
| prevServerAddr | String | N | Previous server address |
| resetSync | Boolean | N | Option to reset synchronization |
| useCustomField | Boolean | N | Option to use custom fields |
| syncMode | String | N | Synchronization mode. `AE` = ADD/UPDATE, `AED` = ADD/UPDATE/DELETE. If null or omitted, defaults to `AED` (ADD/UPDATE/DELETE). |

#### Response Notes:

In the `ADServerSetting` response object, the `password` and `keystorePassword` fields are masked:

- `"password": "****"`
    
- `"keystorePassword": "****"`
    

#### Changes:

- v1.0.2:
    
    - A new optional `syncMode` parameter is now accepted in the request body.
        
    - In the `ADServerSetting` response object, `password` and `keystorePassword` are now **masked** and will always appear as `"****"` instead of the actual values.
        
    - Internal error logging has been strengthened (no API surface change).

This API is used to set the SMTP (Simple Mail Transfer Protocol) for sending emails.

#### BODY Parameters:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| name | String | Y | Enter the SMTP server name. |
| smtp_server_addr | String | Y | Enter the SMTP server address. SMTP server address is the same form as 'smtp. Email Service Provider.com', and you can check it on the settings screen of email to use as an SMTP. |
| smtp_server_port | String | Y | Enter the port number of the SMTP server. you can check it on the settings screen of email to use as an SMTP. |
| smtp_username | String | Y | Enter the account of the SMTP service. |
| smtp_password | String | Y | Enter the password of the SMTP service. |
| smtp_security | String | Y | Select security type. |
| smtp_sender | String | Y | Enter the email address of the sender. |
| description | String | N | Enter the description. |

#### Note:

- When using an SMTP server with an email account that has two-factor authentication (2FA) enabled, be aware of the following:  
    Once 2FA is set up, the SMTP password is **not** the same as the email account password. Instead, it corresponds to an **app password** generated specifically for use with 2FA.  
    If you change the email account password, all existing app passwords are automatically revoked. As a result, the previously configured SMTP password will no longer work.  
    After changing the email account password, you must **generate a new app password** and **update the SMTP settings** with the new value.

request.description:
This API is used to delete Mobile Access Card in Airfob Portal.

There are three variations in using this API:

1. Using Card ID
    
2. Using User ID
    
3. Using ALL parameter on User ID
    

#### BODY Parameters:

| Parameter | Type | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| card_id | Array | Y | Mobile Access Card's card ID |
| user_id | Array | Y | ID of the user which Mobile Access Card will be removed. |

To delete multiple `card_id` or `user_id`, separate each value with comma (,). Ex: \["1", "2", "3"\]

#### Response:

- 200 OK
    

``` json
{
    "failedUsers": [],
    "failedCards": [],
    "mobileUsers": [],
    "status": 0,
    "errorMessage": "",
    "Response": {
        "code": "0"
    }
}

```

- failedUsers : User information that fails after requesting to delete airfob
    
- failedCards : Card information that fails after requesting to delete airfob

item.name: Virtual Device & Event Import

request.description:
Registers a **virtual device** within BioStar X.

Each virtual device:

- Must have a unique `id` (range: 100001–999999).
    
- Cannot duplicate existing device IDs or names.
    
- Appears in device lists when `?virtual=true` is included in search queries.
    

#### BODY Parameters:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| VirtualDevice | Object | Y | Root object containing virtual device details. |
| VirtualDevice.id | Number | Y | Unique identifier for the virtual device. Must be within 100001–999999 and not duplicate any existing device ID. |
| VirtualDevice.name | String | Y | Display name of the virtual device. Must be unique (no duplicate with existing devices). |
| VirtualDevice.description | String | N | Optional text description for internal reference. |
| VirtualDevice.device_group_id | Object | Y | Defines which device group this virtual device belongs to. |
| VirtualDevice.device_group_id.id | Number | Y | The ID of an existing device group. |

#### Notes:

- Virtual devices are **non-functional** placeholders (cannot control doors or perform access).
    
- Once created, the **device ID cannot be modified**.
    
- Virtual devices can be listed using `GET /api/v2/devices/search?virtual=true`.

request.description:
Imports external access events into the system.

Allows batch import of **Access Granted (4088)** and **Access Denied (6400)** events generated by a **Virtual Device**.

This is typically used when an external app generates punch logs (e.g., mobile attendance).

#### Each imported event is stored as a normal BioStar X event, visible in event logs and reports.

Important Rules

- `packet_device_id` and every `events[].dev_id` must match.
    
- Only **virtual devices** are allowed.
    
- Only **event codes 4088 or 6400** are permitted.
    
- `datetime` must be in **UTC** (convert local time accordingly).
    
- All `user_id`s must exist in the system.
    
- Each imported event will be logged individually (one row per event).
    

#### BODY Parameters:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| packet_device_id | Number | Y | Must match `dev_id` of all included events. |
| is_virtual_device | Boolean | Y | Indicates whether the device is virtual. Must be `true`. |
| events | Array | y | List of event objects to import. |
| events.dev_id | Number | Y | Must match `packet_device_id`. |
| events.evt | Number | Y | Event code — only `4088` or `6400` are allowed. |
| events.datetime | String | Y | Event timestamp in UTC. |
| events.user_id | String | Y | Must correspond to a valid user. |
| events.tna_key | String | N | Optional transaction key or reference. |

item.description:
This feature allows external systems (e.g., mobile T&A apps) to send **punch/access events** directly to **BioStar X** **without database-level integration**.

To ensure data consistency, each event must be associated with a **Virtual Device** — a device record created inside BioStar X to represent the external source.

### **Notes:**

- Virtual Devices are **non-functional placeholders** and **cannot be used for access control, doors, or Advanced AC features**. Their only purpose is to logically link imported events to a known device identity.
    
- **BioStar X Event Log API** license is required for the feature.
    

---

### Step-by-Step: How to Use

##### Step 1: Create a Virtual Device

- Use `POST /api/v2/virtual_devices` to create a new virtual device.
    
- Usable ID range: **100001–999999**
    
- Ensure it’s **not a duplicate ID or name**.
    
- Assign it to an existing **device group**.
    

---

##### Step 2: Verify Virtual Device

- Use `GET /api/v2/virtual_devices`
    

to confirm your virtual device is registered and visible.

---

##### Step 3: Import Events

- Prepare your event payload with the same `packet_device_id` as the virtual device ID.
    
- Ensure all timestamps are in **UTC**.
    
- Use `POST /api/events/import` to upload multiple events at once.
    

---

##### Step 4: Verify Event Import

- After import, use existing endpoints such as:
    
    - `POST /api/events/search`
        

to confirm the events are visible in BioStar X event logs.

---

##### Step 5: Troubleshooting

If an error occurs:

- Check that `is_virtual_device` is `"true"`.
    
- Confirm all `user_id`s exist.
    
- Verify datetime formatting (`YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ`).
    
- Ensure the event code is either `4088` or `6400`.

request.description:
You can manage and control the population density of certain areas with Occupancy Limit Zone to prevent overcrowding. You can also monitor the occupancy limit zone status or set an alert to get notified when the zone has reached capacity. Up to 100 occupancy limit zones can be added. #### BODY Parameters: | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | is_enabled | | | Deactivate the occupancy limit zone. If the zone is deactivated, both Count that refers to the number of users in the zone and Count Bypass settings will be initialized. Select Active to enable it. | | name | String | | Enter an occupancy limit zone name. Name can be up to 48 characters in length and cannot be set to the same name as other zones. | | zone_type | Number | | View the zone type. | | status | Number | | | | limit_count | Number | | Enter the number of users who will be restricted from entering the zone. If the number of users in the zone reaches the limit, entry will be restricted. You can enter a number from 0 to 10,000, and if it is set to 0, people can enter the zone without limit. | | entry_devices | Number | | Select a device to use for entry. You can select a device from the list of added devices. | | exit_devices | Number | | Select a device to use at exit. You can select a device from the list of added devices. | | almost_full_alert_setting:level | Number | | Set an alert to an administrator or set to save event logs before Count reaches the occupancy limit. | | almost_full_alert_setting:step_1 | Number | | The first alert for occupancy limit. | | almost_full_alert_setting:step_2 | Number | | The second alert for occupancy limit. | | auto_reset_count_time:is_enabled | Boolean | | Toggle to set the time to automatically reset occupancy limit Count. | | auto_reset_count_time:minute | Number | | The Count will be initialized at the time you set every day. | | network_fail_action | Number | | Set whether to allow users to enter or leave when a network error occurs in the device set in the zone. If you set it to Allow Entry and Exit, it stops entry restrictions when the device loses network connection, and people can enter the zone even if Count in the zone exceeds the occupancy limit. | | bypass_groups:acsgruid | Number | | Set Count Bypass by selecting bypass groups to allow users in the group to enter the zone at any time without affecting the number of Count. The Bypass Count column displays the number of people from Count Bypass in the zone list.
It is not able to use the Count Bypass feature when you set Thermal & Mask Check Mode as Check without authentication while using the device with a thermal camera.
Up to 16 bypass groups can be added. | #### Notes: - Devices and firmware versions that are compatible with the occupancy limit zone features are as follows: - FaceStation F2 firmware version 1.1.0 or later - FaceStation 2 firmware version 1.5.0 or later - A device cannot be assigned to the entry device and exit device simultaneously. - You can set entry devices and exit devices up to 128 in total. - A device using Dual Authentication cannot be set as the entry or exit device. - Set the time of Auto Count Reset based on UTC in the country where the zone is located. For example, if the time is set to 00:00 in UTC+9, the count is automatically reset at 01:00 in UTC+10. - You can set up two alerts for Count Alert and only enter numbers smaller than the Limit value. The values of both alerts must be different.

Anti-passback zone provides an enhanced function than the door based anti-passback feature. #### BODY Parameters: | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | name | String | Y | Enter an anti-passback name. | | description | String | N | Enter an anti-passback description. | | zone_type | Number | Y | View the zone type. | | is_global | Boolean | Y | It is possible to set either Local or Global for the range of zone application. If Local is set, the zone can be set only with the entry devices and devices connected with RS-485, and if Global is set, the zone can be set with all devices enrolled in BioStar X. | | enable | Boolean | Y | Toggle to disable the anti-passback zone. | | status | | N | | | apb_type | Boolean | Y | Select an Anti-passback type. | | reset_time | Number | Y | You can set a time period so that all anti-passback violations can be deleted. This allows the user to be granted access after the time period. The maximum possible duration is 7 days (10080 minutes). If set to 0, anti-passpack violations will not be deleted and the users who have previously violated the anti-passback rule will not be granted access. | | apb_use_door_sensor | Boolean | Y | 0=OFF, 1=ON, 2=Door Config.
You can set the range to apply the anti-passback. If Entry Confirmed APB is set to ON, the anti-passback is applied according to the actual operation of the door that the entry and exit device are configured. If this option is set to OFF, the rule is applied according to the user's authentication regardless of the door operation. When set to Follows door configuration, the anti-passback rule is applied according to the setting of the Use sensor when Entry Confirmed APB enabled option of the door. | | entry_devices:id | Number | Y | Select a device to use for entry. | | exit_devices:id | Number | Y | Select a device to use at exit. | | bypass_groups:id | Number | N | Select an access level. Users who have the access level will not be restricted by the anti-passback rule. | | alarm_actions | Array | N | Choose the operation to be triggered when an APB violation occurs. | | apb_fail_action | Number | N | 0=Open by Auth ,1=Open by Auth & Record APB Log ,2=Door Locked & Record APB Log.
It is possible to set the door operation in case the communication between BioStar X and the device where anti-passback is set has been lost. Setting is available when Global is set for Mode. When Open by auth is set, the door opens when the user has been authenticated normally. When Open by auth & record APB log is set, an anti-passback violation alarm occurs and the door opens. When Door locked & record APB log is set, an anti-passback violation alarm occurs and the door does not open. |

It can be applied either to **all users in the zone** or to a **specific set of users**. #### Body Parameters: | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | ZoneCollection.rows.id | String | Y | The ID of the APB zone to be cleared. | | UserType | Integer | Y | Specifies the clear mode:
1 = Clear APB for all users.
2 = Clear APB for specific users. | | User | Array | N | List of user objects with user_id. Required when UserType = 2. | #### Response Codes: - 200 OK: Request successful - 400 Bad Request (Code 671): Zone ID mismatch (zone not found) - 401 Unauthorized: Login required or invalid session token
