This API is used to create new Fire Alarm Zone. #### BODY Parameters: | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | name | String | Y | Name of the Fire Alarm Zone | | description | String | N | Enter a fire alarm zone description. | | zone_type | Number | Y | View the zone type. | | is_global | Boolean | Y | You can set fire alarm in two different modes. Local mode will allow the master device and slave devices that are connected via RS-485 to be selected. Global mode will allow selection of all devices added to BioStar X. | | enable | Boolean | Y | Toggle to disable the fire alarm zone. | | device_id:id | Number | Y | Add and configure the device to set off the fire alarm signal.
When Local is set for Mode, either Door or Elevator can be set as the fire zone.
When Global is set for Mode, both Door and Elevator can be set as the fire zone at the same time. | | input_index | Number | Y | Input port of the device | | type | Boolean | Y | 0=Normally Open.
1=Normally Closed. | | duration | Number | Y | Duration of the Alarm | | doors:id | Number | N | Select the door(s) to include in the fire alarm zone. | | elevators:id | Number | N | Select the elevators to include in the fire alarm zone. You can select multiple elevators. | | alarm_actions | Array | N | Choose the operation to be triggered when a fire alarm signal occurs. |

The muster zone is used as a place where users gather when an emergency occurs. It can also be used for the purpose of monitoring the number of users and list of users in a specific area, or for notifying the manager of alarms and alerts when a user stays in a specific area for a long time.

#### BODY Parameters:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| name | String | Y | Enter a muster zone name. |
| zone_type | Number | Y | View the zone type. |
| is_global | Boolean | N | You can check the application range of the zone. Only Global mode is supported for muster zone, and the zone can be set with all devices added to BioStar X. |
| enable | Boolean | N | Toggle to disable the muster zone. |
| status | Number | N |  |
| access_groups:id | Number | Y | Set the access group to which the user who will be staying in the muster zone. Up to 16 access groups can be set. |
| entry_devices:id | Number | Y | Select a device to use for entry. |
| exit_devices:id | Number | Y | Select a device to use at exit. |
| time_limit | Number | Y | Set the maximum amount of time that user can stay in the zone. It can be set up to 4320 minutes, and an alarm occurs when the user stays in the muster zone exceeding the specified time. |
| alarm_actions | Array | N | Set the alarm action to carry out when a specific event occurs at the muster zone. |

You can configure the scheduled lock zone. The scheduled lock zone keeps the door locked based on the schedule that has been set. #### BODY Parameters: | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | name | String | Y | Enter a scheduled lock zone name. | | description | String | N | Enter a scheduled lock zone description. | | zone_type | Number | Y | View the zone type | | is_global | Boolean | Y | The scheduled lock zone only supports local mode (0). | | enable | Boolean | Y | Toggle to disable the scheduled lock zone. | | forced_lock:bi_directional_lock | Boolean | Y | 0=Exit allowed.
1=Access not allowed.
You can configure the zone to lock only the entering device, or to lock both entering and exiting device. | | forced_lock:schedule_id:id | Number | Y | Select a schedule.
You can configure a scheduled lock zone by selecting multiple doors in local mode. | | forced_lock:doors:id | Number | Y | Select the door(s) to include in the scheduled lock zone. | | forced_lock:bypass_groups:id | Number | N | Select an access level. Users who have the access level will not be restricted by the scheduled lock rule. | | alarm_actions | Array | N | Select an access level. Users who have the access level will not be restricted by the scheduled lock rule. |

You can configure the scheduled unlock zone. The scheduled unlock zone keeps the door open based on the schedule that has been set. #### BODY Parameters: | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | name | String | Y | Enter a scheduled unlock zone name. | | description | String | N | Enter a scheduled unlock zone description. | | zone_type | Number | Y | View the zone type. | | is_global | Boolean | Y | The scheduled unlock zone only supports local mode (0). | | enable | Boolean | Y | Toggle to disable the scheduled unlock zone. | | status | Number | N | | | isLift | Boolean | Y | You can set doors or elevators as schedule unlock zones. | | first_man_in | Boolean | N | When set as Active (1), the user who belongs to the access group must authenticate in the configured schedule to start a schedule unlock.
Inactive (2). | | schedule_id:id | Number | Y | Select a schedule. | | doors:id | Number | Y | If you select Door, the door list is activated. Select the door(s) to include in the scheduled unlock zone.
You can configure a scheduled unlock zone by selecting multiple doors in local mode. | | first_man_in_groups:id | Number | N | You can select the access group where the user belongs who can start a scheduled unlock. | | alarm_actions | Array | N | Choose the operation to be triggered when a scheduled unlock signal occurs. |

You can configure the scheduled unlock zone. The scheduled unlock zone keeps the door open based on the schedule that has been set. #### BODY Parameters: | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | name | String | Y | Enter a scheduled unlock zone name. | | description | String | N | Enter a scheduled unlock zone description. | | zone_type | Number | Y | View the zone type. | | is_global | Boolean | Y | The scheduled unlock zone only supports local mode (0). | | enable | Boolean | Y | Toggle to disable the scheduled unlock zone. | | status | Number | N | | | isLift | Boolean | Y | You can set doors or elevators as schedule unlock zones. | | first_man_in | Boolean | N | When set as Active (1), the user who belongs to the access group must authenticate in the configured schedule to start a schedule unlock.
Inactive (2). | | schedule_id:id | Number | Y | Select a schedule. | | doors:id | Number | Y | If you select Door, the door list is activated. Select the door(s) to include in the scheduled unlock zone.
You can configure a scheduled unlock zone by selecting multiple doors in local mode. | | lifts:id | Number | Y | If you select Elevator, the elevator list is activated. Select the elevators to include in the scheduled unlock zone. You can select multiple elevators. | | lifts:floors:id | Number | Y | You can select the floor of the selected elevator. | | lifts:control_device_id:id | Number | Y | Input the elevator control device id. | | first_man_in_groups:id | Number | N | You can select the access group where the user belongs who can start a scheduled unlock. | | alarm_actions | Array | N | Choose the operation to be triggered when a scheduled unlock signal occurs. |

When intrusion alarm zone is used, you can detect trespassing of an unauthorized user to a designated zone without permission. #### BODY Parameters: | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | name | String | Y | Enter an intrusion alarm zone name. | | description | String | N | Enter an intrusion alarm zone description. | | zone_type | Number | Y | Input the zone type. | | is_global | Boolean | Y | Application range of the zone. Only Local mode is supported for intrusion alarm zone, and the zone can be set only with devices connected to the entry device and RS-485. | | enable | Boolean | N | Toggle to disable the intrusion alarm zone. | | status | Number | N | | | arm_delay | Number | Y | Delay time from the authentication to the arm | | disarm_delay | Number | Y | Delay time from the intrusion detection to the alarm occurs. | | doors:id | Number | Y | Select the doors to include in the intrusion alarm zone. | | access_groups:id | Number | N | You can add an access group with permission to arm or disarm. You can register up to 128 access groups. | | cards:id | Number | Y | You can add a card with permission to arm or disarm. You can register up to 128 access cards. | | arm_inputs:device:id | Number | Y | You can set the arming and disarming by device or input signal.
Select a device to control the intrusion alarm zone among the entry and exit devices of the door | | arm_inputs:arm_type | String | Y | Option: arm, disarm, toggle | | arm_inputs:input_type | String | Y | Option: card, input | | arm_inputs:input_index | Number | Y | Input port of the selected device. | | arm_inputs:duration | Number | Y | Duration which the device sensor connected to arm/disarm. | | arm_inputs:normal_open | Boolean | Y | Default door status | | intrusion_inputs:device:id | Number | Y | Select a device to control the intrusion alarm zone among the entry and exit devices of the door | | intrusion_inputs:input_index | Number | Y | Input port of the selected device. | | intrusion_inputs:duration | Number | Y | The device recognizes intrusion if N/O sensor connected to input port n for the set duration. | | intrusion_inputs:normal_open | Boolean | Y | Default Door status | | intrusion_inputs:input_type | String | Y | input | | intrusion_actions | Array | N | Set the alarm action to carry out when a specific event occurs at the intrusion alarm zone. | #### Notes: - `arm_inputs`, `intrusion_inputs`, `intrusion_actions` is activated only when Door is set from Configuration.

Interlock zone monitors the status of two or more doors by door sensor and relay state to control that one door cannot be opened or close if other doors are open or unlocked. You can also disable access if a user stays within the zone.

- An interlock zone can be configured with up to 4 doors.
    
- An interlock zone can only set the doors with the devices connected to the CoreStation.
    
- A device set as an interlock zone cannot be set to another zone.
    
- A door set as an interlock zone cannot be set to another zone other than the fire alarm zone.
    

#### BODY Parameters:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| name | String | Y | Enter an interlock zone name. |
| description | String | N | Enter an interlock zone description. |
| zone_type | Number | Y | View the zone type. |
| is_global | Number | Y | You can check the application range of the zone. Only Local mode is supported for interlock zone, and the zone can be set only with devices connected to the CoreStation and RS-485. |
| enable | Number | Y | You can disable the interlock zone. input 1 to enable it. |
| inter_lock:doors:id | Number | Y | Select the doors to include in the interlock zone. You must select at least two doors that are the door sensor is connected. |
| sensor_inputs:device:id | Number | Y | If a user stays in the zone, this option can prevent others from entering the zone. Input the device ID which will be used as sensor. |
| sensor_inputs:input_index | Number | Y | Index of the sensor which will be used. |
| sensor_inputs:duration | Number | Y | Set the duration. |
| sensor_inputs:normal_open | Number | Y | Set status between Normally Open or Closed. |
| sensor_inputs:input_type | String | Y | Type of the input. |
| alarm_actions | Array | N | Set the alarm action to carry out when a specific event occurs at the interlock zone. |

request.description:
In order to view event log through Suprema BioStar X API, you will need a Session ID which will be available after you’re logged in.

To log in, make a form encoded server side POST request as shown on example below.

`POST /api/login`

`Host: server_ip|domain_name[:port]`

`{“User”: {“login_id”: “user_login_id_here”, “password”: “password_here”}}`

The parameters are:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| login_id | String | Y | Your login ID |
| password | String | Y | Your password |

If successful, you will receive back the following response:

``` json
Status: 200 OK
bs-session-id: c52127fea7f645149083d04d89ad68e5
Content-Type: application/json;charset=UTF-8
{"User":{"user_id":"1","name":"Administrator","gender":"1","email":"admin111@gmail.com","birthday":"1977-10-08T04:00:00.00Z","photo_exists":"false","pin_exists":"false","login_id":"admin","password_exists":"true","updated_count":"15","last_modified":"946","idx_last_modified":"263","start_datetime":"2001-01-01T00:00:00.00Z","expiry_datetime":"2030-12-31T23:59:00.00Z","security_level":"0","display_duration":"20","display_count":"3","permission":{"id":"1","name":"Administrator","description":"this is a permission for Administrator","filter":{"UserGroup":["1"],"DeviceGroup":["1"],"DoorGroup":["1"],"ElevatorGroup":["1"],"ZoneType":["-1"],"AccessGroup":["0"],"GraphicMapGroup":["1"]},"module":{"Dashboard":{"read":"true","write":"true"},"User":{"read":"true","write":"true"},"Device":{"read":"true","write":"true"},"Door":{"read":"true","write":"true"},"Elevator":{"read":"true","write":"true"},"Zone":{"read":"true","write":"true"},"AccessControl":{"read":"true","write":"true"},"Monitoring":{"read":"true","write":"true"},"TA":{"read":"true","write":"true"},"Setting":{"read":"true","write":"true"},"Video":{"read":"true","write":"true"},"Visitor":{"read":"true","write":"true"}},"device":{"id":"542071155","id":"543720368","id":"939257394"},"user":{"id":"1","id":"2","id":"3","id":"4","id":"5","id":"6","id":"7","id":"20","id":"21","id":"22","id":"23","id":"24","id":"25","id":"26","id":"27","id":"28","id":"29","id":"30","id":"31","id":"32","id":"33"}},"inherited":"false","user_group_id":{"id":"1","name":"All Users"},"disabled":"false","expired":"false","department":"department 12","user_title":"title","idx_user_id":"1001","idx_user_id_num":"1001","idx_name":"2001","idx_phone":"1001","idx_email":"5001","fingerprint_template_count":"0","face_count":"0","card_count":"0","access_groups":[{"id":"1","name":"Meeting Room 1 AG"},{"id":"2","name":"Meeting Room 2 AG"},{"id":"22","name":"Building Access"}],"user_custom_fields":[{"user_id":{"user_id":"1","name":"Administrator"},"custom_field":{"id":"1","name":"Test","type":"0","order":"1"}},{"user_id":{"user_id":"1","name":"Administrator"},"custom_field":{"id":"2","name":"33","type":"0","order":"2"}}],"need_to_update_pw":"false"},"Response":{"code":"0","link":"https:\/\/support.supremainc.com\/en\/support\/home","message":"Success"}}

```

There will be a key called bs-session-id, which is a consecutive strings of hex digits located on the header of the response:  
`bs-session-id: 643f64efb7114485a2fd89de17ca13fb`

This value will be used as authorization of the APIs that will be called. To apply this value, put it on the header of the API that will be called with key name `bs-session-id`.

After logging in, you can use this API to view event log(s). #### BODY Parameters: | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | limit | Number | N | Limit the response records by the value stated on this parameter | | conditions:column | String | | Field which the conditions will be based from | | conditions:operator | Number | | Condition Operator
EQUAL : 0,
NOT_EQUAL : 1,
CONTAINS : 2,
BETWEEN : 3,
LIKE : 4,
GREATER : 5,
LESS : 6 | | conditions:values | Datetime | | Value of the conditions | | orders:column | String | | Field which the sorting will be based on | | orders:descending | Boolean | | Toggle true to set the sorting as descending | There are two main components on this API: conditions and orders. On conditions, you can put multiple criteria to filter the API response according to your need. While on orders, it is used to sort the response according to specified parameter, either ascending or descending. On this example, there are three criteria on the conditions component: - First criteria filter the response according to datetime parameter (column: datetime) with date range between (operator: 3) value specified on values: \[\]. - Second criteria filter the response to show event log where the device id (column: device_id) is not (operator: 1) as specified (values \[\]). - Third criteria filter the response to show event logs which have the specified value (values \[\]) as its id (column:id, operator: 0). - The relation between multiple criteria on a single API call is AND. For the orders component on this example, it will sort the response according to parameter datetime (column: datetime) ascending (descending: false). Do note that the datetime that is shown on the response is based on UTC-0, so if you want to view event log from a device which use UTC+8, then you need to convert the datetime from UTC+8 to UTC-0 and use the conversion result as criteria value on the conditions. ##### Example: - Device: UTC+8. Event log occurred when datetime on the device is 2022-06-02 01:37 - Convert the datetime to UTC-0 : 2022-06-01 17:37 - Use this converted value as criteria value on the condition

This example shows how to view event log from device with id: 543308153 (device timezone set as UTC+8) and log with datetime between 2022-06-01 00:05 to 2022-06-30 23:58.

Note that the datetime used on the API criteria is different from what the user would like to see. This is because the device was set to use UTC+8, so to view the correct log, use datetime which have been converted to UTC-0.

request.description:
Next step on creating a new user it to enroll the credential that the user will be using. Either it's Card, Face, Visual Face or Fingerprint.

This API is used to enroll card credential using the specified device.

Keep the card_id value on the response which shows after running this API to apply it to the user which will be created.

To view device(s) with capability to enroll a card, use `View Device(s) Based on Enrollment Type` API.

query.description: Set the sensitivity for the position, angle, and distance of a face when registering the face. Set the sensitivity high if you wish to obtain a detailed face template. 0-9

variable.description: ID of the device which will be used to enroll

request.description:
Use this API to enroll face credential from a device.

Set the pose_sensitivity to 0 if you're doing a Visual Face scan.

Keep the response of this API after running to apply it to the user which will be created.

To view device(s) with capability to enroll Face/Visual Face, use `View Device(s) Based on Enrollment Type` API.

Use this API to scan a fingerprint.

On calling this API, it is required to specify the device which will be used as fingerprint scanner. It is done by specifying Device ID on the API Path, which marked by :dev_id.

The procedure to add a fingerprint as follows:  
\- Scan a finger using this API  
\- Use `POST /api/server_matching/identify_finger` to check if it's already exist  
\- If it's not exist, then run a second scan using this API for the same finger  
\- Compare 1st and 2nd scan result using `POST /api/devices/:dev_id/verify_fingerprint` to make sure that 1st and 2nd scan are the same finger  
\- After running `Verify Fingerprint Scan`, keep the value of template 0 and 1 to assign the enrolled finger to a user using `POST|PUT /api/users/:id`

#### BODY Parameters:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| ScanFingerprintOption |  |  |  |
| :enroll_quality | Number | Y | Set a fingerprint enrollment quality level. Any fingerprint which does not meet the quality requirement will not be enrolled. |
| :raw_image | Boolean | Y | Set this option to view the original image when a fingerprint is scanned. |
| noblockui | Boolean | Y |  |

After preparing available user_id and credential(s), use this API to create a new user using the basic configuration. For applying configuration beyond this, such as credential(s) or custom fields, refer to the subsequent example. #### BODY Parameters: | **Parameters** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | user_id | String | Y | ID of the User. Unique.

When User ID Type in Setting > Server is set to Number, a number between 1 and 4294967295 can be entered. When Alphanumeric is set, a combination of alphabetic characters and numbers can be entered.
Do not use spaces when entering ID.
Numbers or Alphanumeric characters can be set for user_id. For more details, refer to Setting Section of this documentation. | | name | String | N | Name of the user. Up to 48 characters. | | user_group_id | Number | Y | ID of the User Group that the user will be under. Refer to User Group Section of this documentation for listing available User Groups. | | disabled | Boolean | N | Toggle User active or not; false for active, true for non-active. | | start_datetime | Date/Time | Y | Start active period for the User. | | expiry_datetime | Date/Time | Y | End active period for the User. | | email | String | N | Email address of the User. | | department | String | N | Department of the User. Up to 64 characters. | | title | String | N | Title of the User. | | photo | Base64 | N | Photo of the User. | | phone | String | N | Phone number of the User. | | permission | Number | N | Operator Level of the User.

**None**: The user has no operator privilege. To use this Operator Level, remove permission parameter from the API Request.
**Administrator**: The user can use all menus.
**User Operator**: The user can only use the USER and PREFERENCE menus.
**Monitoring Operator**: The user can use the MONITORING and PREFERENCE menus and only view the DASHBOARD, USER, DEVICE, DOOR, ZONE and ACCESS CONTROL menus.
**Video Operator**: The user can only use the VIDEO menu.
**T&A Operator**: The user can only use the TIME ATTENDANCE menu and only view the USER menu.
**User**: The user can only view own information and T&A records. | | access_groups | Number | N | ID of the Access Group that the User will have access. Refer to Access Group Section of this documentation for listing available Access Groups.
Can enter more than 1 Access Group. | | login_id | String | N | Required when permission parameter is not removed. | | password | String | N | Required when permission parameter is not removed. | | user_ip | String | N | Limit the access of the User so he/she can only login from the registered IP Address.

The user IP can be entered in the format xxx.xxx.xxx.xxx. Each octet can only be entered in numbers between 0 and 255.
Users whose user IP is not registered can log in from any IP. | | pin | Number | N | PIN of the user. Maximum 32 digit. | When Setting > Server is set to Number, you can make use of endpoint `/api/users/next_user_id` to automatically get the next user_id. Refer to `GET` Find Next Available UserID for the detail of this endpoint. #### Known errors: - name is more than 48 characters - Using single quote on name - Duplicate user_id, email, or login_id - Empty user_id, user_group_id, start_datetime, expiry_datetime - Using user_group_id:id which doesn't exist - Putting "/" at the end of the endpoint - Password with 3 or more subsequent letter or number - Start and expiry date difference is more than 30 years

Use `GET /api/setting/custom_fields` to view available Custom Fields.

There are 3 type of Custom Fields: Text Input, Number Input and Combo Box. Text Input and Combo Box will accept any value, and Number Input will only receive number.

Body parameter for Creating/Updating Custom Field on a User:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| item | String | N | Content of the Custom Field |
| custom_field:id | Number | Y | ID of the Custom Field |

To skip create/update a Custom Field, remove it from the Body.

item.name: Step 5b User Credential: Misc (Include Device Default Authentication Mode)

request.description:
On this example you can see how to use stated parameters to adjust misc. credential values.

Parameters on this example:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| pin | Number | N | PIN of the User |
| security_level | Number | N | To set the security level of the credential. The higher the security level is set, the false rejection rate (FRR) gets higher, but the false acceptance rate (FAR) gets lower. Acceptable value: 0 to 5 |
| private_operation_modes |  |  |  |
| :operation_method | Number |  | The first credential of an Authentication Mode (0: ID, 1: Card, 2: Fingerprint/Biometric (Standard), 3: Face) |
| :operation_mode | Number |  | Authentication Mode combination (see below for reference, "-1" = do not use) |
| :exclude | Boolean |  | If Include Device Default Authentication Mode option is on, so when you want to also follow the Auth Modes that the devices have, “private_operation_modes” will have same number of elements that you actually configured. But if Exclude Device Default Authentication Mode is on, you need to add four or more elements because all the values of “operation_method” should be included to override device’s Auth Modes. |

item.name: Step 5c User Credential: Misc (Exclude Device Default Authentication Mode)

request.description:
As stated on previous example (User Credential: Misc (Include Device Default Authentication Mode)), when you want to use Exclude Device Default Authentication Mode then you need to state all values of the Authentication Method.

On this example, the Authentication Mode that's being used is Biometric (operation_method: 2) only (operation_mode: 0) and the other Authentication Method: ID (0), Card (1), and Face (3) are set as do not use (operation_mode: -1) and hidden (exclude: true)

To use Extended Auth Mode simply use code that starts from 11 (refer to Authentication Mode Combination Table on User Credential: Misc (Include Device Default Authentication Mode)), do note that this mode only supported by FaceStation F2.

This example shows how to assign a card to a User, which is applicable to CSN, Wiegand, Smart, Mobile, and QR/Barcode.

First the card must be enrolled to the database by using `POST /api/cards`.

After enrolled, use `GET /api/cards` to view all Cards or `GET /api/cards/unassigned` to view all Cards which are unassigned.

Select a card from the response and apply it on the body as shown on the example to assign it to the selected user.

This example shows how to add a Face Credential on a User.

You can add the user's faces if the device supports face authentication.

- When registering a face, maintain a distance of 40cm to 80cm between the device and the face.
    
- Be careful not to change the facial expression. (smiling face, drawn face, wink, etc.)
    
- If you do not follow the instructions on the screen, the face registration may take longer or may fail.
    
- Be careful not to cover the eyes or eyebrows.
    
- Do not wear hats, masks, sunglasses or eyeglasses.
    
- Be careful not to display two faces on the screen. Register one person at a time.
    
- It is recommended for a user wearing glasses to register both faces with and without glasses.
    

Parameters on this example:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| faces | Array | Y | Main container of the credential |
| :raw_image | Raw | Y | Raw image of the face scan |
| :templates | Array | Y | Container of the face templates |
| ::template | Raw | Y | Face template of the User |

To get data for parameter raw_image and template, use `GET /api/devices/:device_id/credentials/face`

Use `POST /api/v2/devices/search` to list all device which can support the selected credentials. See API documentation for details on the usage of the API.

When updating a User, it is required to put the credential data as acquired from `GET /api/users/:id`  
This is done so the data won't be lost after update.

To delete a Face, skip the parameters on using this API.

You can use raw_image value as value on photo parameter of a User.

Visual Face is a credential that captures the user's face with a visual camera. It is different from face information captured with an infrared camera and is only available on devices that support Visual Face. There are two ways to input a Visual Face: by scanning through a enrollment device, or by uploading an image. Up to two (2) visual faces can be added. Use `GET /api/devices/:device_id/credentials/face` to scan Visual Face using an enrollment device. You can enroll a visual face using FaceStation F2. Use `POST /api/v2/devices/search` to list compatible devices. To upload image as a Visual Face: image file size is up to 10MB and supported file formats are JPG, JPEG and PNG. Use `PUT /api/users/check/upload_picture` to check whether the image is suitable for use or not. BODY example on using Upload Picture: ``` json { "User": { "credentials": { "visualFaces": [{ "template_ex_picture": "/9j/4AAQSkZJRgABAQAAAQABAAD/ Truncated" }] }} } ``` Parameters on this example: | **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | visualFaces | Array | Y | Main container of Visual Face credential | | :template_ex_normalized_image | Base64 | Y | Cropped image ready for visual face extraction. | | :templates | Array | Y | Container of the templates | | ::credential_bin_type | Number | Y | UNKNOWN = -1
FACE_TEMPLATE = 0
FACE_TEMPLATE_IMAGE = 1
FACE_RAW_IMAGE = 2
FACE_TEMPLATE_EX_VER2 = 5 (For FaceStation F2)
FACE_TEMPLATE_EX_VER_3 = 9 (For BioStation 3 and W3)
FACE_TEMPLATE_EX_NORMALIZED = 7
FACE_TEMPLATE_EX_PICTURE = 8 | | ::template_ex | Raw | Y | Visual Face template data which includes FaceStation F2 and the new BioStation 3. | | :template_ex_picture | Base64 | Y | Parameter for Upload Picture raw data |

This example shows how to update fingerprint data on a User.

The BODY parameters as follows:

| **Parameter** | **Type** | **Required** | **Description** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| fingerprint_templates |  |  |  |
| :finger_mask | Boolean | N | Set to True to mark the fingerprint as a duress fingerprint. When threatened by someone to open the door, the user can authenticate using this fingerprint to send an alarm signal to BioStar X. |
| :template0 | Raw | Y | First scan template of the fingerprint |
| :template1 | Raw | Y | Second scan template of the fingerprint |
| :isNew | Boolean | N |  |

Use `POST /api/devices/:dev_id/scan_fingerprint` to enroll fingerprint  
Use `POST /api/server_matching/identify_finger` to check whether the fingerprint already recorded or not.

- Raw data for template0 and template1 available after enrolling a fingerprint.
    
- Fingerprint(s) used for regular access should not be registered as duress fingerprint.
    
- If the fingerprint authentication rate is low, delete the existing fingerprint information and add a new fingerprint.
    
- Use an adequate security level. If 1:1 Security Level is too high, the fingerprint authentication rate may be too low or the false rejection rate (FRR) may be too high.
    
- For best fingerprint scanning quality, make sure to cover the entire surface of the fingerprint sensor with the finger.
